problem_model.system_description: Policy engine with deterministic rule evaluation using regex patterns and structured conditions. Supports Gate actions (requiring prior actions within timeframe) and Ensure actions (script-based verification). No NLP or network dependencies - regex and structured conditions only. First-match-wins evaluation with comprehensive condition functions including parameter checks, spending limits, credential verification, and recent action history. Features encrypted vault with tiered access (unencrypted ledger, session-encrypted state, compartment-encrypted credentials), Argon2id key derivation, device-specific session key caching, and preflight constraint tracking with violation escalation.

failure_modes.description: ASK constraints with broad conditions fire on EVERY tool call touching related files, causing excessive permission prompts
Policy HMAC verification failure falls back to safe defaults
Invalid JSON input to test command
Invalid regex patterns in tool_pattern cause rules to be silently skipped during compilation
Condition evaluation errors cause rules to not match, falling through to default action
Large input sizes (1M+ characters) may impact evaluation performance
Homoglyph attacks using Unicode lookalikes bypass string-based protections
Path traversal attempts in tool names don't cause crashes but may bypass intended restrictions

failure_modes: Vault lockout after 5 failed passphrase attempts for 5 minutes
Expired credentials return None during retrieval
Session key decryption failure when device identifiers change
Policy HMAC verification fails when policy modified without re-signing
Database corruption could cause vault operations to fail
Missing device identifiers fall back to generic string
Credential capability requests fail when constraints don't match
Preflight HMAC mismatch causes preflight to be ignored with warning
dependencies: Vault for action history and credential storage
File system permissions (Unix mode 0o600 for session keys)
assumptions: Agents will submit structured plans before execution
Pre-commitment mechanisms reduce goal pressure during planning
Default policy path is ~/.signet/policy.yaml
Home directory expansion works
Vault passphrase is at least 8 characters
SIGNET_SESSION environment variable for session-specific controls
Tool calls have structured JSON parameters
Rule evaluation budget is under 10ms for performance
First matching rule wins - rule ordering matters
Locked rules cannot be auto-fixed and protect core system functionality
Self-protection rules are always first in evaluation order
Ensure scripts are placed in ~/.signet/checks/ directory
Gate actions fail closed when vault is unavailable
Session TTL is 30 minutes (1800 seconds)
Maximum 5 failed vault unlock attempts before 5-minute lockout
Maximum 20 preflight constraints per preflight
Default violation threshold of 5 before escalation
AES-256-GCM provides sufficient encryption
Argon2id default parameters provide adequate protection
Device identifiers remain stable across reboots
SQLite database integrity is maintained
File system supports Unix permissions on supported platforms
boundaries: Policy file must be YAML format
No NLP or network operations in policy evaluation
Regex patterns only - no code execution in conditions
Script names must be simple filenames without path separators
Self-protection rules cannot be disabled or bypassed
Vault tiers: Tier 1 unencrypted (ledger), Tier 2 session-encrypted (state), Tier 3 compartment-encrypted (credentials)
Session key cache encrypted with device key, not plaintext
Failed attempt lockout limited to 5 minutes
Preflight constraints capped at 20 per submission
Device key derivation uses machine ID and username only
One-time credentials invalidated after first successful use
history: Plan B alternatives implemented for existing rules
success_shape: Aviation-inspired safety mechanisms
Deterministic enforcement
Pre-execution planning reduces risk
Structured mission plans with identified risks and constraints
Comprehensive CLI with clear subcommands
Secure vault operations with passphrase confirmation
Graceful fallback to safe defaults on policy tampering
Flexible pause/resume controls at global, rule, or session level
Evaluation completes under 10ms performance budget
Self-protection rules prevent policy tampering
Gate actions enforce proper sequencing of operations
Ensure actions enable external script validation
Comprehensive condition functions for flexible rule authoring
Auto-fix capability for common policy errors while preserving locked rules
Graceful degradation on condition evaluation errors
Three-tier security model with appropriate encryption levels
Device-specific session key caching for seamless hook mode operation
Brute-force protection with progressive lockout
Credential scoping by domain, purpose, amount, and one-time usage
Preflight constraint tracking with violation counting and escalation
HMAC-based integrity protection for policies and preflights
Graceful fallback when device identifiers unavailable
Comprehensive audit logging of all vault operations
acceptance_criteria: cargo build --release works
cargo test passes (88 tests)
Policy YAML loading functions correctly
HMAC-signed vault-stored mission plans supported
Policy engine evaluates 1000+ rules under 10ms
Self-protection rules cannot be bypassed by any tool call
Gate actions correctly check vault action history
Ensure actions return unresolved for external script execution
Validation identifies and can auto-fix regex errors, timeout ranges
Default policy blocks known dangerous operations (rm, force push, etc.)
Locked rules are preserved during auto-fix operations
Vault setup and unlock with passphrase works correctly
Credential storage and retrieval across all tiers functions properly
Session spending tracking and limits enforced accurately
Recent action queries support pipe-delimited OR searches
Policy HMAC signing and verification prevents tampering
Preflight storage, violation tracking, and escalation work as designed
Device key encryption/decryption of session keys successful
Failed attempt lockout and recovery timing correct
Credential capability requests respect all metadata constraints
One-time credentials properly invalidated after use
